schedule._note: Pre-retirement: brokerage-only before age 59.5 (IRS early withdrawal gate)
Retirement gap: between retirement (65) and RMD start (75)
RMD era: RMDs supplement withdrawals; amount_k is above-RMD discretionary spending

readme.purpose: Annual take-home withdrawal targets — the cash deposited to your bank account, post-tax. Taxes (federal, state, NIIT) are computed separately and debited from brokerage on top of this amount. Enter what you want to spend, not a grossed-up number.

format.ages: Recommended. Use exclusive, non-overlapping age ranges: '47-64' means ages 47 through 64 inclusive. The loader converts to simulation years using current_age from person.json (year N = age N - current_age). Overlapping ranges cause a validation error.
format.years: Legacy format. Year numbers are simulation-relative: year 1 = current_age + 1. Still supported for backward compatibility but not recommended — ages format is self-documenting.
format.exclusive_ranges: Ranges must not overlap. Use '47-64' and '65-74' (not '47-65' and '65-74'). The validator will raise an error if ranges overlap.
format.mixed: Each row can independently use 'ages' or 'years' — they can coexist in the same schedule.

validation_rules.no_overlap: Age ranges must not overlap. Error raised if two rows share any age.
validation_rules.age_order: age_end must be > age_start. Error raised if reversed or equal.
validation_rules.base_k_le_amount_k: base_k must be <= amount_k. Error raised if floor exceeds target.
validation_rules.past_ages: Ages at or before current_age are silently skipped with a log warning.

fields.floor_k: Global minimum take-home in $K regardless of market conditions. Simulator will not scale below this even in severe downturns. Should be <= all row base_k values.
fields.amount_k: Target take-home in $K in today's purchasing power (inflation-adjusted annually). This is what you want deposited to your bank account — not grossed up for taxes.
fields.base_k: Minimum acceptable take-home for this age range in $K. Models spending flexibility. Set lower if you can cut spending in bad markets; set equal to amount_k for fixed spending.
fields._note: Documentation field — ignored by the simulator. Use to annotate life-stage meaning.

readme.pre_59_5_rule: Before age 59.5, ALL withdrawals are automatically sourced from Brokerage only. IRA and Roth withdrawals incur a 10% IRS early withdrawal penalty — the simulator enforces this hard gate regardless of withdrawal_sequence settings in economic.json.

example_non_overlapping.comment: Three life stages: working / retirement gap / RMD era
